channel.title: cs.GL updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: cs.GL updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: The Reconstructions of Konrad Zuse's Z3 Computer
item.description: arXiv:2607.13089v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper reviews the various reconstructions of Konrad Zuse's Z3 computer, built in 1941. The first is the reconstruction made by Zuse himself in the 1960s, which is now housed at the Deutsches Museum in Munich. Second is the 2001 reconstruction, made in Berlin, and which has a didactic purpose. Third is the Z3r, a Z3 reconstruction built by Horst Zuse in honor of his father's 100th birthday. Fourth is the reconstruction by Christoph Traber in Switzerland. All of these machines strive to preserve the cultural heritage that the Z3 represents.
item.dc:creator: Raul Rojas
